phenomenon: category size bias in probability judgment
constructs: category membership salience category-level probability individual outcome likelihood
claim: Individual-level judgments inherit category-level properties when category information is psychologically salient, biasing outcome likelihood estimates upward for larger categories.

phenomenon: category elimination as progress proxy in hedonic management
constructs: category structure subjective progression valence-dependent strategy
claim: Consumers use category elimination as a progress marker, preserving categories in positive experiences and eliminating them in negative experiences to alter perceived rate of progression.

phenomenon: processing fluency-decision context fit effect on evaluation intensity
constructs: numeric fluency decision mode congruence evaluation amplification
claim: Fluency-decision fit induces feeling-right experience that intensifies product evaluations in their original direction rather than shifting judgment valence.

constructs: imaginability fictionality authorial authority moral deviance
claim: Audiences experience multiple interrelated puzzles when authors attempt to make morally or conceptually deviant propositions true in fictional worlds via authorial say-so.

phenomenon: genre-modulated imaginative resistance to moral deviance
constructs: genre conventions genre expectations moral deviance contextual acceptance
claim: Identical morally deviant propositions evoke less resistance when presented in genres whose conventions permit moral divergence from reality, suggesting context-dependency overlooked by proposition-focused theories.
